news.Title: Microchip or Fabrinet: Which Tech Stock Is a Better Buy Now?
news.Content: Two companies currently drawing significant investor interest in the Zacks Computer and Technology sector are Microchip Technology MCHP and Fabrinet FN. Microchip Technology develops, manufactures and sells smart, connected and secure embedded control solutions. The company focuses on general-purpose and specialized 8-bit, 16-bit and 32-bit mixed-signal microcontrollers, microprocessors, analog, FPGA and memory products. Microchip now offers 64-bit mixed-signal microprocessors, expanding its footprint beyond the 32-bit architecture. Fabrinet is a provider of advanced optical packaging and precision optical, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microchip Technology (MCHP) and Fabrinet (FN) are highlighted as significant players in the Zacks Computer and Technology sector.
• MCHP is benefiting from AI investments, with strong sales growth in its Gen 4 and Gen 5 data center products and the launch of a 3nm-based PCIe Gen 6 switch.
• Fabrinet is experiencing growth from automation and AI, serving as a key manufacturing partner for advanced optical transceivers and HPC clusters.
• Fabrinet expects Q4 fiscal 2026 revenues between $1.25 billion and $1.29 billion, supported by its debt-free balance sheet.
news.Keywords: embedded control solutions, optical packaging, AI infrastructure, data center products, manufacturing services

news.Title: Lam Research (LRCX): A Strategic Investment in the Transformative Industry
news.Content: Sands Capital Management, LLC released its Q1 2026 investor letter for its “Select Growth Strategy”. A copy of the letter is available to download here . Select Growth mainly targets leading U.S. businesses, driving positive structural changes. U.S. large-cap growth stocks fell in the first quarter. Sharp dispersion driven by AI advances marked the quarter, but late in the quarter, geopolitical tensions with Iran caused a broad-based risk-off move across the market. AI continued to influence market behavior, with ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) was added as a new position by Sands Capital Select Growth Strategy in Q1 2026.
• LRCX is a leading provider of semiconductor fabrication equipment, with market share leadership in leading-edge etch applications, primarily for memory and secondarily for logic chips.
• The company is well-positioned to benefit from a multiyear investment cycle driven by the industry's transition to advanced nodes and increasing demand for memory to support AI applications.
• LRCX's revenue for the March quarter of 2026 was $5.84 billion, a 9% sequential increase and a 24% increase year-over-year.
• Hedge fund holdings in LRCX increased in Q1 2026, with 123 portfolios holding the stock.
news.Keywords: semiconductor fabrication equipment, etch applications, AI memory demand, multiyear investment cycle, market share leader

news.Title: Why Tower Semiconductor Soared in May
news.Content: Shares of specialty semiconductor foundry Tower Semiconductor (NASDAQ: TSEM) rallied 15.5% in May, according to data from S&P Global Market Intelligence . Tower delivered strong first-quarter earnings on May 13, and also disclosed that it had received significant orders out to 2028, with customers even ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) shares rallied 15.5% in May, driven by strong first-quarter earnings and significant orders extending to 2028.
• The company reported Q1 revenue growth of 15.6% to $414 million and adjusted EPS growth of 62.9% to $0.57, both beating analyst expectations.
• Tower secured $1.3 billion in silicon photonics contracts for 2027 and received customer prepayments to secure capacity, with larger commitments for 2028.
• Susquehanna analyst significantly increased the price target to $330 from $180, projecting an $8 EPS run rate by 2028.
• Tower's leadership in silicon photonics technology positions it as a key player in the AI sector, with Nvidia investing heavily in the space.
news.Keywords: specialty foundry, silicon photonics, revenue growth, customer prepayments, AI superstar

news.Title: Lumentum Stock Falls 15% in a Month: Is This Dip a Buying Opportunity?
news.Content: Lumentum Holdings ’ LITE shares have declined 14.9% over the past month, significantly underperforming the broader Zacks Computer and Technology sector’s modest 0.2% decline. The pullback reflects investor concerns surrounding supply-chain bottlenecks, execution risks tied to aggressive AI-related capacity expansion and persistent weakness across non-AI businesses despite strong quarterly results. Investors also remain cautious about elevated AI infrastructure CapEx, rising inventory levels and large-scale manufacturing expansion initiatives, which could pressure near-term profitability and execution. In addition, continued softness in industrial lasers and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Lumentum Holdings (LITE) shares have declined 14.9% over the past month, underperforming the tech sector.
• Investor concerns include supply-chain bottlenecks, execution risks from AI capacity expansion, and weakness in non-AI businesses.
• Elevated AI infrastructure CapEx, rising inventory, and large-scale manufacturing expansion are also pressuring near-term profitability.
• Despite headwinds, Lumentum is strengthening its position in Optical Interconnect, Data Center Networking, Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), and Advanced Laser Chip technologies.
• The company secured a multi-hundred-million-dollar purchase order for CPO applications, with meaningful revenue contribution expected by December 2026.
news.Keywords: supply chain, AI capacity, CPO, laser chip, optical interconnect

news.Title: ADI Climbs 77.4% in a Year: Time to Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
news.Content: Analog Devices ADI shares have climbed 77.4% in a year, outperforming the Zacks Computer and Technology sector’s appreciation of 43.5%. The rise in stock price is due to the restoration of investors’ confidence in a broad-based recovery in ADI’s industrial segment and AI-related data center demand, driving higher utilization and a favorable mix. ADI 12-Month Performance Chart Image Source: Zacks Investment Research The rise in stock price has resulted in ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Analog Devices (ADI) shares have risen 77.4% in a year, driven by industrial segment recovery and AI data center demand.
• ADI's industrial segment revenue grew 56% year-over-year to $1.80 billion in Q2 fiscal 2026, representing 50% of total revenue.
• Communications revenue increased 79% year-over-year to $554.7 million, with data centers accounting for over 75% of this segment.
• Despite competition, ADI's margins expanded, with expected adjusted operating margin around 49% for Q2 fiscal 2026.
• ADI is trading at a forward P/S multiple of 12.54X, significantly higher than the sector average of 6.59X.
news.Keywords: semiconductor, industrial demand, AI data center, revenue growth, margin expansion

news.Title: Stocks Climb on an AI-Led Rebound
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) today is up +0.93%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) is up +0.89%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) is up +1.17%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) are up +0.76%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) are up +0.92%. Stock indexes are climbing for a second day today as the rebound in the artificial intelligence trade continues. Strength in chipmakers and AI-infrastructure stocks is leading gainers in the broader market. Also, today’s -2% decline in WTI crude oil prices (CLN26) has knocked inflation expectations lower and is supportive for stocks and bonds. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Major stock indexes (S&P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq 100) are up, led by a rebound in AI-related stocks, particularly chipmakers and AI infrastructure companies.
• Declining WTI crude oil prices are supporting stocks and bonds by lowering inflation expectations.
• US trade deficit narrowed in April to -$55.9 billion, and China's May exports and imports exceeded expectations.
• Lam Research (LRCX), KLA Corp (KLAC), and Applied Materials (AMAT) are among the top-performing chipmakers.
• Interest rate futures indicate a low probability of a rate hike at the upcoming FOMC meeting.
news.Keywords: AI rebound, chipmakers, inflation expectations, trade deficit, interest rates

news.Title: 4 Solid Stocks to Buy as S&P 500 Hits Fresh All-Time Closing High
news.Content: Wall Street has had an impressive run this year despite geopolitical tensions and a spike in inflation that has time and again raised concerns. The S&P 500, particularly, has been one of the biggest gainers this year, with the index hitting a fresh all-time closing high on Monday. The index is getting a boost from optimism surrounding AI and an impressive performance by mega-cap tech stocks. Given the ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Analog Devices (ADI) is highlighted as a solid stock to buy, with an expected earnings growth rate of 59.3% for the current year.
• The Zacks Consensus Estimate for ADI's current-year earnings has improved by 9.1% in the past 60 days.
• Semiconductor stocks are performing exceptionally well, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index up around 73.6% year-to-date.
• Goldman Sachs projects the S&P 500 to gain 12% in 2026.
news.Keywords: S&P 500, AI optimism, semiconductor stocks, earnings growth, Zacks Rank

news.Title: 00916連日溢價 專家勸：不要亂買 實際配息可能大幅下降
news.Content: 國泰全球品牌 50 ETF(00916-TW) 於本月 1 日發布收益分配期前公告後，引爆市場搶買熱潮，導致市價大幅溢價。為保障既有受益人權益並收斂溢價差，國泰投信於 8 日在官網發布重要公告，提醒投資人務必留意溢價風險及後續 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 國泰投信提醒投資人注意溢價風險，並表示實際配息金額以第二次公告為準，可能因申購贖回狀況而調整。
• 多位理財專家呼籲投資人切勿盲目追價，應審慎評估風險。
• 專家認為近期市場波動加劇，應靜待市場情緒回穩，並關注具備平台優勢、品牌影響力及穩定客群的大型企業。

The company is currently pursuing the integration of on-device generative AI into all of its product lines. Silicon Motion is a leading developer of microcontroller ICs for NAND flash storage devices. The semiconductor ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Qualcomm (QCOM) and Silicon Motion (SIMO) are key semiconductor firms with exposure to smartphones, automotive, AI, and data storage.
• Qualcomm is integrating on-device generative AI into its product lines and sees traction in edge networking and automotive V2X communication.
• Silicon Motion is a leading supplier of microcontroller ICs for NAND flash storage devices and expects its 3D SSD controllers to be a growth driver.
• Qualcomm faces competition from Intel in the AI PC market and expects near-term softness due to OEM inventory management and US-China trade tensions.
• Silicon Motion is well-positioned to adapt to industry changes through collaborations with flash vendors.

news.Title: 5 Stocks to Boost Your Portfolio on Soaring Semiconductor Sales
news.Content:
Investors have lately been rotating out of tech stocks and taking refuge in defensive assets, as a surge in oil prices owing to the continued tensions in the Middle East has raised fears of inflation rising further. However, the enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence has continued to help AI-focused companies.

High demand for AI chips has been boosting semiconductor stocks, which have primarily been responsible for the broader market rally over the past three years. Given this scenario, it would be ideal to invest in semiconductor stocks, such as Analog Devices ADI, NXP Semiconductors NXPI, Microchip Technology MCHP, Texas Instruments TXN and ASML Holding N.V. ASML, which have great potential for growth this year.

Semiconductor Revenues Surging Steadily

Global semiconductor sales totaled $110.5 billion in April, jumping 11% sequentially from March’s total of $99.5 billion, the Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA) reported. Year over year, semiconductor sales soared 93.9% $56.9 billion in April 2025.

“Global semiconductor sales increased on a month-to-month basis for the 14 th consecutive month in April, and the global market continues to notch robust year-to-year growth driven by sales into the Asia-Pacific region, the Americas, and China,” John Neuffer, SIA president and CEO, said.

The robust jump in April follows a stellar first quarter when semiconductor sales totaled $298.5 billion, increasing 25% sequentially.

Semiconductors have become a key part of nearly every modern and developing technology, from IoT and 6G to artificial intelligence. Growing demand from the automotive sector has also helped boost chip sales. Meanwhile, the fast-growing AI market — which is still in its early stages — is prompting major technology companies to invest billions of dollars in development and infrastructure.

With big tech firms continuing to spend aggressively on AI infrastructure, investor confidence in semiconductor companies has remained strong.

The SIA also backed the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics’ (“WSTS”) Spring 2026 global semiconductor sales forecast. The WSTS projects the global semiconductor market to grow 90% this year, reaching $1.51 trillion.

5 Semiconductor Stocks With Upside

Analog Devices

Analog Devices is an original equipment manufacturer of semiconductor devices, specifically analog, mixed-signal and digital signal processing (“DSP”) integrated circuits. ADI’s product line comprises amplifiers and comparators; analog to digital converters; digital to analog converters; video encoders and decoders; embedded processing products and DSPs; MEMS and temperature sensors; RF/IF components and converters; power and thermal management ICs, audio/video converters, amplifiers, CODECs, filters and processors. Analog Devices also offers analog, digital and RF switches and multiplexers; analog microcontrollers; clock and timing products.

Story Continues

Analog Devices’ expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 59.3%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 0.1% over the past 60 days. ADI currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

NXP Semiconductors

NXP Semiconductors provides high-performance, mixed-signal and standard product solutions that leverage its RF, analog, power management, interface, security and digital processing expertise. NXPI seems well-positioned to capitalize on the level 2-5 automotive market. Additionally, NXP Semiconductors is the leader in general-purpose microcontrollers and application processors in industrial and IoT markets.

NXP Semiconductors’ expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 25.1%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 5.7% over the past 60 days. NXPI carries a Zacks Rank #2 at present.

Microchip Technology

Microchip Technology has been consistently benefiting from its strength in the analog and microcontroller businesses. MCHP’s dominance in 8, 16, and 32-bit PIC microcontrollers remains a major driver of top-line and bookings growth. Microchip Technology has acquired notable companies like Tektronix, Microsemi and Atmel to add strength to its product offerings.

Microchip Technology’s expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 84.2%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 18% over the past 60 days. MCHP currently has a Zacks Rank #1.

Texas Instruments

Texas Instruments is an original equipment manufacturer of analog, mixed-signal and digital signal-processing integrated circuits. TXN has manufacturing and design facilities, including wafer fabrication and assembly/test operations in North America, Asia and Europe. Management strategy has been to build assets that would be fully utilized through their lifetimes and outsource any excess demand in peak situations to outside foundries.

Texas Instruments’ expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 40.6%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 20.6% over the past 60 days. Currently, TXN carries a Zacks Rank #2.

• 港股方面，恆指收跌1.22%，報24657.06點，權重科技股如百度、美團、阿里巴巴、京東、小米、騰訊均下跌。
news.Keywords: A股,恆指,指數下跌,成交量縮減,板塊輪動

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: 《美股》升息恐慌+殖利率飆 Meta摔 費半崩10%
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-06-08 06:23:17 郭妍希 發佈

5月就業報告遠優、引發市場擔憂聯準會(Fed)的貨幣政策立場恐轉趨鷹派，近來紅火的AI晶片股應聲崩跌，美國四大指數5日全面重挫。

道瓊工業平均指數6月5日終場 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美國5月就業報告遠優於預期，引發市場擔憂聯準會(Fed)可能轉趨鷹派，導致美股四大指數全面重挫。
• 道瓊工業平均指數下跌1.35%，那斯達克指數重挫4.13%，標準普爾500指數下挫2.46%，費城半導體指數慘崩10.26%。
• 費城半導體指數創下2020年3月以來最大單日跌幅，市值蒸發超過1兆美元。
• AI晶片股如博通、美光、輝達、台積電ADR均出現顯著下跌，Meta Platforms亦重挫5.51%。
• 市場擔憂聯準會可能升息，公債殖利率應聲跳高，2年期公債殖利率創2025年2月以來新高。

news.Title: Is Rambus Inc. (RMBS) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
news.Content: Is RMBS a good stock to buy? We came across a bullish thesis on Rambus Inc. on Valueinvestorsclub.com by IdeaLogue. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on RMBS. Rambus Inc.'s share was trading at $148.02 as of May 28 th . RMBS’s trailing P/E was 70.47 according to Yahoo Finance. Photo by ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Rambus Inc. (RMBS) is presented as a potentially undervalued beneficiary of the accelerating AI infrastructure buildout, particularly due to rising CPU demand and upcoming MRDIMM chipset.
• The company's business model includes highly profitable patent licensing and silicon IP segments, generating recurring revenue, alongside a rapidly growing product segment.
• Rambus has significantly increased its market share in register clock drivers (RCDs) for server memory modules from zero in 2018 to approximately 45% currently.
• The MRDIMM technology represents a substantial market opportunity, with management estimates suggesting consensus forecasts are conservative; successful execution could lead to significant upside.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, intellectual property licensing, memory interface, market share expansion, earnings potential

news.Title: There’s Still Time to Invest in Lumentum’s Optical Networking Momentum
news.Content: Earlier this week, shares of Lumentum (LITE) rallied from about $806 to $1,049. Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang praised Marvel (MRVL), a maker of optical components, as the next potential trillion-dollar company. Not only did that spark a massive rally in Marvell shares, but it also sparked a rally in other optical-component stocks, like LITE. Investors are rushing to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Lumentum (LITE) experienced a significant stock rally, driven by its role in optical networking essential for the AI boom, offering higher bandwidth and speed than copper wiring.
• Nvidia has committed $2 billion to Lumentum through a multi-year agreement to accelerate advanced optical technologies and secure capacity for AI factories.
• The optical networking market is projected to grow substantially, from an estimated $15 billion in 2026 to nearly $154 billion by 2028, driven by AI demand.
• In its most recent quarter, Lumentum reported adjusted EPS of $2.37 (vs. $2.27 est.), revenue of $808.4 million (vs. $810M est.), and an adjusted gross margin of 47.9% (vs. 45% est.).
• The company provided strong forward guidance, expecting revenue between $960 million and $1.01 billion, exceeding estimates, and EPS between $2.85 and $3.05, also above expectations.
news.Keywords: optical networking, AI infrastructure, supply-demand imbalance, revenue growth, forward guidance
